steps.title: Recognize the fractional exponent rule
steps.content:
The expression $(x)^{\frac{1}{17}}$ has a fractional exponent. The key rule for fractional exponents states: when the exponent is $\frac{1}{n}$, the denominator becomes the root index and the numerator stays as the power.

Applying this with $n = 17$:

$$(x)^{\frac{1}{17}} = \sqrt[17]{x}$$

This is the 17th root of $x$ — the number that, when raised to the 17th power, equals $x$.

steps.title: Identify the equivalent expression
steps.content:
Now compare $\sqrt[17]{x}$ against each choice:
  • A) $\frac{x}{17}$ — This divides $x$ by 17, not a root operation
  • B) Represents the 17th root of $x$
  • C) $x = x^1$ — The exponent is 1, not $\frac{1}{17}$
  • D) $x^{17}$ — This is raising to the 17th power, which is the inverse of taking a 17th root
That matches choice B — B is correct.
